description: Locked reference outputs for ADR-009 parity validation
llama_cpp_invocation: llama-completion (NOT llama-cli) with hf2q's rendered chat template
llama_invocation_recipe: llama-completion -m <gguf> -f <rendered_prompt> -n <N> --temp 0 --top-p 1.0 --top-k 1 --repeat-penalty 1.0 --no-warmup -sp --no-perf -no-cnv --jinja; strip echo via sed '/<channel|>/,$p' + drop everything up to and including '<channel|>' (matches scripts/adr022_p18_byte_equal.sh)
refresh_note: 2026-05-16 full refresh: previous (2026-05-01) anchor was against gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-ara-abliterated-dwq.gguf (DWQ-trained quant), whose model dir is no longer present locally. The session that surfaced this ran scripts/release-check.sh on the present model (gemma4-ara-2pass-APEX-Q5_K_M.gguf, a different quantization variant of the same base architecture) and saw 0/6 parity checks pass because the stale refs encoded a different model's argmax outputs — NOT because of a kernel regression. Cross-validation: scripts/adr022_p18_byte_equal.sh confirmed hf2q output and llama-completion output are byte-identical on the APEX-Q5_K_M model for the short_hello prompt (`2 + 2 = 4<turn|>`). Both refs (*_hf2q.txt + *_llama.txt) were re-captured against APEX-Q5_K_M on hf2q HEAD 4166ce50 / llama-completion build d05fe1d7d. Long-prompt divergence between independent kernel implementations (hf2q mlx-native vs llama.cpp ggml-metal) is a structural baseline: FP non-associativity cascading through argmax tie-breaks at ~byte 117 (sourdough) and ~byte 129 (sliding_wrap). Both outputs are independently coherent (cross-verified end-to-end). scripts/parity_check.sh floors recalibrated to current measurements (short_hello 16, sourdough 117, sliding_wrap 129) so future kernel changes that further reduce divergence point trip the regression gate.
divergence_note: Independent kernel implementations diverge on word-choice argmax flips, NOT on coherence breakdown. Both outputs are valid English completing the prompt. Cross-implementation argmax flips on tight tokens are inherent to independent kernel arithmetic; closing them is ADR-015 mlx-native parity territory.

sourdough.vs_llama_status: PASS — common_prefix 117 ≥ 117 floor (anchored to today's measurement)

short_hello.note: Divergence at byte 16 is EOS marker only — hf2q ends at `<turn|>` (16 B), llama continues to `<turn|> [end of text]` (30 B). hf2q output is the full prefix of llama output. Content identical (`2 + 2 = 4`). Cross-validated byte-equal on the APEX-Q5_K_M model via scripts/adr022_p18_byte_equal.sh.

sliding_wrap.vs_llama_status: PASS — common_prefix 129 ≥ 129 floor (anchored to today's measurement)

gate_h_open_issue.summary: Gate H (TQ-active quality envelope) fails on APEX-Q5_K_M with cosine_mean=0.865 vs floor 0.999. The frozen Gate H fixture (sourdough_tq_quality.json) was captured against the DWQ model and shows cosine_mean=0.9996 (TQ effectively matches dense on DWQ).
gate_h_open_issue.operator_question: TQ KV cache is designed to be model-agnostic — cosine 0.865 on APEX is a real quality signal, not threshold tuning. Investigation needed: why does TQ on APEX-Q5_K_M deviate this much from dense when TQ on DWQ matches dense at 0.9996?
gate_h_open_issue.deferred_to: Next mission — do NOT relax Gate H thresholds to mask this. Gate H fixture intentionally NOT refreshed so the 0.9996 envelope remains a high-bar reference for the investigation.
